Title: Successful Login
Preconditions: User has a valid username and password.
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid username and password.
2. User clicks login button.
3. API Manager receives login request.
4. API Manager verifies credentials.
5. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives a successful login response.

Title: Invalid Username
Preconditions: User has an invalid username but a valid password.
Test Steps:
1. User enters invalid username and valid password.
2. User clicks login button.
3. API Manager receives login request.
4. API Manager verifies credentials.
5. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives an unsuccessful login response.

Title: Invalid Password
Preconditions: User has a valid username but an invalid password.
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid username and invalid password.
2. User clicks login button.
3. API Manager receives login request.
4. API Manager verifies credentials.
5. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives an unsuccessful login response.

Title: Empty Username
Preconditions: User leaves the username field empty.
Test Steps:
1. User leaves the username field empty.
2. User enters valid password.
3. User clicks login button.
4. API Manager receives login request.
5. API Manager verifies credentials.
6. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives an unsuccessful login response due to empty username.

Title: Empty Password
Preconditions: User leaves the password field empty.
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid username.
2. User leaves the password field empty.
3. User clicks login button.
4. API Manager receives login request.
5. API Manager verifies credentials.
6. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives an unsuccessful login response due to empty password.

Title: Create API
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks create API button.
2. User enters API name and version.
3. User clicks submit button.
4. API Manager receives create API request.
5. API Manager creates the API.
6. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives a successful API creation response.

Title: Delete API
Preconditions: User is logged in and an API exists.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks delete API button.
2. User enters API name and version.
3. User clicks submit button.
4. API Manager receives delete API request.
5. API Manager deletes the API.
6. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives a successful API deletion response.

Title: Handle Request
Preconditions: User is logged in and an API exists.
Test Steps:
1. User sends a request to the Service.
2. Service receives the request.
3. Service processes the request.
4. Service sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives a successful request handling response.

Title: Generate Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and an API exists.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks generate report button.
2. User enters report name.
3. User clicks submit button.
4. API Manager receives generate report request.
5. API Manager generates the report.
6. API Manager sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives a successful report generation response.

6. API Manager encounters an error.
7. API Manager sends error response back to User.

Title: Service Loop
Preconditions: User is logged in and an API exists.

2. Service receives the request.
3. Service processes the request.
4. Service sends response back to User.
5. User sends another request to the Service.
6. Service receives the request.
7. Service processes the request.
8. Service sends response back to User.
Expected Result: User receives a successful request handling response for both requests.
